statement.en: Stabilizing Quantum Gravity (SQG): Emergent Spacetime, Gauge Symmetry, and Matter from Recoverable Quantum Codes
statement.summary: This document presents the Stabilizing Quantum Gravity (SQG) research program: a unified quantum-information and operator-algebraic framework in which spacetime, gravity, gauge structure, matter, chirality, and cosmological dynamics emerge from a deeper recoverable logical substrate. Crucially, this release marks the transition of SQG from a structural framework into a computational, predictive architecture. SQG no longer merely proposes mechanisms; it outputs finite, checkable numerical benchmarks across particle physics and cosmology. At its core, SQG advances a radical but disciplined claim: Physical reality is not fundamentally built from pre-given spacetime, primitive particles, or externally imposed gauge laws. It is built from recoverable, stabilizable logical structure. In this vie

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The SQG framework is presented as a 2026 preprint claiming computational transition from structural to predictive architecture. It proposes novel unification (spacetime from quantum codes, emergent gauge symmetry, matter from logical substrate) with claimed 'finite, checkable numerical benchmarks,' but these benchmarks are not presented in the abstract itself. The work is dated April 2026 (future from training cutoff) and explicitly frames itself as transitional ('no longer merely proposes'). Status is provisional—requires empirical validation, peer review, and publication of the promised numerical predictions before classification can shift toward TRUE or FALSE. The radical ontological claim (reality from 'recoverable logical structure') lacks consensus validation.
